name: Keeping the pace
description: Whether speedrunning or in slow motion, explore how running builds tension, emotion and excitement on screen.

key_work.title: Celeste
key_work.brief_description: Celeste (2018) is a game about continuing onwards, even when things are tough. Created by Noel Berry and Maddy Thorson just before Thorson’s gender transition, the game is a metaphor for a young woman facing struggles with mental health and identity, represented as one great task: climbing a mountain.

constellations_primary.name: Keeping the pace
constellations_primary.description: Whether speedrunning or in slow motion, explore how running builds tension, emotion and excitement on screen.

start.title: Celeste
start.brief_description: Celeste (2018) is a game about continuing onwards, even when things are tough. Created by Noel Berry and Maddy Thorson just before Thorson’s gender transition, the game is a metaphor for a young woman facing struggles with mental health and identity, represented as one great task: climbing a mountain.

end.title: Run Lola run = Lola Rennt [DVD]
end.creator_credit: Tom Tykwer, Stefan Arndt, Arte, Bavaria Film, BMI, FFA - Filmforderungsanstalt, FilmFernsehFonds Bayern, German Independents, NRW, WDR and X filme creative Pool
end.brief_description: Time is running out for Lola (Franka Potente). She’s just received a frantic phone call from her boyfriend Manni (Moritz Bleibtreu), who’s lost a small fortune in cash belonging to his Mob boss. Now she has to run for his life, to try and come up with the money before Manni pays the ultimate price for his mistake. Using a destiny device, the story begins anew with different outcomes. Other cinematic techniques which add to the breakneck pace and excitement of this innovative thriller include jump cuts, whip pans, slow and fast motion, split-screen, intercut colour and black and white, segment titles and animation.

constellations_other.name: Keeping the pace
constellations_other.description: Whether speedrunning or in slow motion, explore how running builds tension, emotion and excitement on screen.

end.creator_credit_for_label: Tom Tykwer, Stefan Arndt, Arte, Bavaria Film, BMI, FFA - Filmforderungsanstalt, FilmFernsehFonds Bayern, German Independents, NRW, WDR and X filme creative Pool

links.description: Speedrunning Celeste parallels the experience of watching Run Lola Run. Featuring a protagonist with similarly distinctive red hair, the film draws on videogame conventions as we experience Lola’s 20-minute run through Berlin three times.

end.title: Mission: Impossible III
end.creator_credit: J.J. Abrams, Colleen Atwood, Maryann Brandon, Mary Jo Markey, Paula Wagner, Tom Cruise, Zhao Haicheng, Cruise/Wagner Productions, Paramount Pictures, Scott Chambliss, Alex Kurtzman and Roberto Orci
end.brief_description: Retired from active duty, and training recruits for the Impossible Mission Force, agent Ethan Hunt faces the toughest foe of his career: Owen Davian, an international broker of arms and information, who’s as cunning as he is ruthless. Davian emerges to threaten Hunt and all that he holds dear – including the woman Hunt loves.

constellations_other.name: Keeping the pace
constellations_other.description: Whether speedrunning or in slow motion, explore how running builds tension, emotion and excitement on screen.

end.creator_credit_for_label: J.J. Abrams, Colleen Atwood, Maryann Brandon, Mary Jo Markey, Paula Wagner, Tom Cruise, Zhao Haicheng, Cruise/Wagner Productions, Paramount Pictures, Scott Chambliss, Alex Kurtzman and Roberto Orci

links.description: Run Lola Run and Mission: Impossible III use a ticking clock to heighten the tension in running sequences, as these films’ protagonists race through a city to save their partners.

start.title: Mission: Impossible III
start.creator_credit: J.J. Abrams, Colleen Atwood, Maryann Brandon, Mary Jo Markey, Paula Wagner, Tom Cruise, Zhao Haicheng, Cruise/Wagner Productions, Paramount Pictures, Scott Chambliss, Alex Kurtzman and Roberto Orci

end.title: Severance
end.creator_credit: Adam Scott, Jessica Lee Gagné, Sean Fogel, Wei-Ning Yu, Animals & People, Endeavor Content, Fifth Season, Red Hour and Westward
end.brief_description: Mark leads a team of office workers whose memories have been surgically divided between their work and personal lives. When a mysterious colleague appears outside of work, it begins a journey to discover the truth about their jobs.

constellations_other.name: Keeping the pace
constellations_other.description: Whether speedrunning or in slow motion, explore how running builds tension, emotion and excitement on screen.

end.creator_credit_for_label: Adam Scott, Jessica Lee Gagné, Sean Fogel, Wei-Ning Yu, Animals & People, Endeavor Content, Fifth Season, Red Hour and Westward

links.description: For the opening running sequence in Severance Season 2, actor Adam Scott was influenced by Tom Cruise’s upright, straight palm style of running in films such as Mission: Impossible III.

start.title: The Worst Person in the World
start.creator_credit: Joachim Trier, Ellen Ystehede, Olivier Bugge Coutté, Andrea Berentsen Ottmar, Thomas Robsahm, ARTE France Cinéma, B-Reel Films, Film i Väst, MK2 Films, MK Productions, Oslo Pictures, Snowglobe, Roger Rosenberg and Eskil Vogt
start.brief_description: The chronicles of four years in the life of Julie, a young woman who navigates the troubled waters of her love life and struggles to find her career path, leading her to take a realistic look at who she really is.

constellations_other.name: Keeping the pace
constellations_other.description: Whether speedrunning or in slow motion, explore how running builds tension, emotion and excitement on screen.

start.creator_credit_for_label: Joachim Trier, Ellen Ystehede, Olivier Bugge Coutté, Andrea Berentsen Ottmar, Thomas Robsahm, ARTE France Cinéma, B-Reel Films, Film i Väst, MK2 Films, MK Productions, Oslo Pictures, Snowglobe, Roger Rosenberg and Eskil Vogt

end.title: Ferris Bueller's day off [DVD]
end.creator_credit: John Hughes, Tom Jacobson and Paramount Pictures
end.brief_description: A 1961 Ferrari 250 GT California. It was one man’s love, his passion, and his fault he didn’t lock the garage when Ferris Bueller wanted the day off. One day, towards the end of his senior year, Ferris (Matthew Broderick), cuts school and heads for downtown Chicago with his girl Sloane and his best friend Cameron, to show what a little ingenuity, a lot of nerve, and a red Ferrari can do. From John Hughes comes a contemporary comedy which follows Ferris’ wildly audacious and nearly flawless plan to take it easy. Featuring a soundtrack from artists including Yello, Big Audio Dynamite, Sigue Sigue Sputnik, The Beat, The Beatles and The Dream Academy. Also stars Alan Ruck and Mia Sara. Cinematography, Tak Fujimoto. Script, John Hughes.

constellations_other.name: Keeping the pace
constellations_other.description: Whether speedrunning or in slow motion, explore how running builds tension, emotion and excitement on screen.

end.creator_credit_for_label: John Hughes, Tom Jacobson and Paramount Pictures

links.description: Director Joachim Trier noted that the dreamlike running sequence in The Worst Person in the World was influenced by the playful ‘Twist and Shout’ scene in Ferris Bueller’s Day Off.

start.title: Ferris Bueller's day off [DVD]
start.creator_credit: John Hughes, Tom Jacobson and Paramount Pictures
start.brief_description: A 1961 Ferrari 250 GT California. It was one man’s love, his passion, and his fault he didn’t lock the garage when Ferris Bueller wanted the day off. One day, towards the end of his senior year, Ferris (Matthew Broderick), cuts school and heads for downtown Chicago with his girl Sloane and his best friend Cameron, to show what a little ingenuity, a lot of nerve, and a red Ferrari can do. From John Hughes comes a contemporary comedy which follows Ferris’ wildly audacious and nearly flawless plan to take it easy. Featuring a soundtrack from artists including Yello, Big Audio Dynamite, Sigue Sigue Sputnik, The Beat, The Beatles and The Dream Academy. Also stars Alan Ruck and Mia Sara. end.title: The Darjeeling Limited
end.creator_credit: Wes Anderson, Jeremy Dawson, Jacqueline Getty, Milena Canonero, Andrew Weisblum, Lydia Dean Pilcher, Roman Coppola, Scott Rudin, American Empirical Pictures, Collage, Fox Searchlight Pictures, Mark Friedberg and Jason Schwartzman
end.brief_description: Three American brothers who have not spoken to each other in a year set off on a train voyage across India with a plan to find themselves and bond with each other — to become brothers again like they used to be. Their “spiritual quest”, however, veers rapidly off-course (due to events involving over-the-counter pain killers, Indian cough syrup, and pepper spray).

constellations_other.name: Keeping the pace
constellations_other.description: Whether speedrunning or in slow motion, explore how running builds tension, emotion and excitement on screen.

end.creator_credit_for_label: Wes Anderson, Jeremy Dawson, Jacqueline Getty, Milena Canonero, Andrew Weisblum, Lydia Dean Pilcher, Roman Coppola, Scott Rudin, American Empirical Pictures, Collage, Fox Searchlight Pictures, Mark Friedberg and Jason Schwartzman

links.description: Like characters in The Darjeeling Limited running to catch the leaving train, the concluding running sequence in Ferris Bueller’s Day Off similarly combines humour, excitement and anxiety as Ferris runs through his neighbourhood to make it home in time.

start.title: The Darjeeling Limited
start.creator_credit: Wes Anderson, Jeremy Dawson, Jacqueline Getty, Milena Canonero, Andrew Weisblum, Lydia Dean Pilcher, Roman Coppola, Scott Rudin, American Empirical Pictures, Collage, Fox Searchlight Pictures, Mark Friedberg and Jason Schwartzman

Their “spiritual quest”, however, veers rapidly off-course (due to events involving over-the-counter pain killers, Indian cough syrup, and pepper spray).

constellations_other.name: Keeping the pace
constellations_other.description: Whether speedrunning or in slow motion, explore how running builds tension, emotion and excitement on screen.

start.creator_credit_for_label: Wes Anderson, Jeremy Dawson, Jacqueline Getty, Milena Canonero, Andrew Weisblum, Lydia Dean Pilcher, Roman Coppola, Scott Rudin, American Empirical Pictures, Collage, Fox Searchlight Pictures, Mark Friedberg and Jason Schwartzman

end.title: Chariots of fire
end.brief_description: Historical drama based on a true story of two members of Britain’s 1924 Olympic team, Harold Abrahams, a Jew running to prove himself in the face of the subtle hostility he found in his British environment, and Eric Liddell, a deeply religious man who refused to run on the Sabbath. Academy Award winner for Best Picture.

constellations_other.name: Keeping the pace
constellations_other.description: Whether speedrunning or in slow motion, explore how running builds tension, emotion and excitement on screen.

links.description: Similar to the iconic running sequence in Chariots of Fire, The Darjeeling Limited also relies on slow motion to elevate the emotion in the physical act of running.

links.description: Lola in Run Lola Run experiences a time-loop, having three attempts to get 100,000 Deutschmarks to her partner within 20 minutes. Time is similarly distorted in The Worst Person in the World in a scene where Julie runs through a time-frozen Oslo.
